name: Aeronca Chief
source: Aeronca 11AC Chief Pilot's Operating Manual / FAA TCDS A-781
notes: 1946-era CAR-3 design; per FAA TCDS A-781, Vne is 122 mph (level flight maximum operational placard). Note that TCDS also lists higher 'glide or dive' calibrated speed (171 mph CAS) but the operating Vne placard is 122 mph. Stored 122 matches MPH placard verbatim. Vno of 91 mph stored confirms MPH convention. Needs conversion ÷1.15078 = 106 KIAS.

name: Aviat Husky A-1C
source: Aviat Aircraft Husky A-1C specifications; multiple POH sources
notes: Modern 2000s-era POH uses KIAS. Listed Vne for A-1C variants is 132 KIAS (some sources show 145 mph max speed, but Vne placard is in knots). Stored value not yet populated; should be set to 132 KIAS. No conversion needed.

name: Beechcraft Musketeer
source: AOPA Beechcraft Sport fact sheet; A23-19/19A POH (Beechcraft 169-590002-7)
notes: 1970s Musketeer A23/B19 POH was dual-marked (KIAS and MPH columns). Official Vne is 152 KCAS = 175 MPH. Stored 156 does not match either exactly (152 kt or 175 mph). Stored value appears to be 156 mph (from a 1965-1967 Musketeer A23 owner's manual which uses MPH). Likely needs conversion 156 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 135 KIAS, or stored should be replaced with canonical 152 KIAS. Defer to canonical 152 KIAS.

name: Beechcraft Sierra
notes: 1973-1976 B24R Sierra 200 POH uses dual MPH/KIAS markings. Official Vne is 152 KCAS = 175 MPH. Stored 183 does not match either; possibly a typo or different year variant. Closest interpretation: 183 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 159 KIAS. However, canonical Vne for the type is 152 KIAS — stored value appears erroneous. Treat as mph if conversion needed (= 159 KIAS), but recommend cross-check; preferred canonical 152 KIAS.

name: Beechcraft Sundowner
source: Beechcraft Sundowner 180 C23 POH (M-1285 and after), Section II Airspeed Limitations
notes: POH directly read: Vne = 152 KIAS = 175 MPH (dual-column table). Stored 179 does not match either. Possible legacy from earlier B23 (1969-1972) variant. Best interpretation: stored value is mph (closer to 175 than 152). 179 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 156 KIAS. Recommend canonical 152 KIAS.

name: Bellanca Super Viking
notes: Modern Bellanca 17-30A POH lists Vne as 196 KIAS (226 mph). Stored 195 matches knots within rounding. No conversion needed.

name: CAP 232
source: EASA TCDS EASA.A.072 CAP 22/231/232/232EX; CAP 232 manufacturer documentation
notes: Modern French aerobatic monoplane certified under EASA. POH and EASA TCDS report Vne in knots. Stored 220 matches knots. No conversion needed.

name: Cessna 162
notes: Light Sport Aircraft (ASTM F2245-based), 2009-era POH uses KIAS exclusively. Vne = 146 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Cessna 177
notes: Cessna 177 Cardinal (1968-1978, 177 and 177A/B) Owner's Manual lists Vne = 185 MPH redline (CAS). Stored 179 does not match either as MPH or knots directly. Likely the stored value corresponds to a different variant year or an interpretation of Vno (which is 145 mph in original). Interpretation: assume stored is mph from an older variant. 179 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 156 KIAS. Recommend canonical 161 KIAS (185 mph).

name: Cessna 177RG
notes: 1971-1978 Cardinal RG owner manual uses MPH/CAS dual markings. Vne is approximately 180 MPH CAS (= ~156 KIAS). Stored 179 closely matches MPH. Cessna switched to knots circa 1976; older POHs used mph. Treat as mph: 179 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 156 KIAS.

name: Cessna 206
notes: Modern (1978+ U206G and later) POH uses KIAS. Vne approximately 174 KIAS (varies slightly by year/variant). Stored matches knots. No conversion needed.

name: Cessna 210
notes: Later models (210N, 210R) Vne = 200 KIAS. Cessna switched to knots c. 1976. Stored 200 matches knots. Earlier (1960-1975) variants used MPH (e.g., 1962 210B at ~200 mph), but modern published value is 200 KIAS. No conversion needed for modern data.

name: Cessna 310R
notes: 1975+ Cessna 310R POH uses KIAS. Vne = 223 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Cirrus SR20
notes: Modern Cirrus SR20 POH lists Vne = 200 KIAS (204 KCAS). Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Cirrus SR22
notes: Modern Cirrus SR22 POH lists Vne = 201 KIAS (204 KCAS). Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Cirrus SR22T
source: Cirrus SR22T G5/G6/G7 POH Section 2 Limitations
notes: Cirrus SR22T POH lists Vne = 205 KIAS (208 KCAS) up to 17,500 ft MSL. Vne reduces linearly to 175 KIAS at 25,000 ft. Stored matches sea-level Vne. No conversion needed.

name: Diamond DA20-A1
notes: Modern Austrian/Canadian aircraft, POH uses KIAS. Vne = 164 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Diamond DA20-C1
notes: DA20-C1 POH Vne = 164 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Diamond DA40-180
notes: Official DA40-180 AFM Vne = 178 KIAS. Stored 181 does not match — appears to be a transcription error. POH uses knots only. Recommend canonical 178 KIAS, no unit conversion needed (already in knots).

name: Diamond DA40-NG
notes: Modern DA40 NG (Austro AE300 diesel) Vne = 172 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Diamond DA42-L360
notes: DA42 L360 (Lycoming) variant POH Vne = 194 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Diamond DA42-NG
notes: DA42 TDi/NG Vne = 194 KIAS per most POH revisions (note: newer 'DA42 NG VI' variant may be 188 KIAS). Stored 194 matches. No conversion needed.

name: Diamond DA62
notes: Official DA62 AFM Vne = 205 KIAS. Stored 194 appears erroneous (possibly carried over from DA42 value). POH uses knots only. Recommend canonical 205 KIAS, no unit conversion.

name: Ercoupe 415C
source: Ercoupe 415-C Operator Handbook; Ercoupe checklist (Vne 144 mph / 125 kts)
notes: Confirmed via Ercoupe checklist PDF: Vne = 144 mph (125 kts). Stored 140 is close but does not match — likely from earlier 75 hp 415-CD variant or rounded down. CAR-3 era aircraft, published in MPH. If treating stored as mph: 140 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 122 KIAS. Recommend canonical 125 KIAS (144 mph).

name: Evektor SportStar
notes: LSA/CS-LSA aircraft, modern POH uses knots. Vne approximately 138 KIAS (varies slightly by sub-variant). Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Extra 300
notes: German aerobatic aircraft, EASA-certified, modern POH uses knots. Vne = 220 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Extra 300L
notes: Extra 300L variant Vne = 220 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Extra 330SC
source: Extra 330SC POH; AOPA Gold Standard article
notes: Extra 330SC POH Vne = 220 KIAS, ±10 G load factor. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Extra NG
source: Extra NG POH; EASA TCDS EASA.A.595
notes: Modern Extra NG (composite single-seat) Vne approximately 221 KIAS per EASA TCDS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Flight Design CTLS
notes: LSA, POH uses knots. Vne = 145 KIAS (some POH revisions list 146). Stored matches within rounding. No conversion needed.

name: Focke-Wulf Fw 190 A-8
source: Bedienungsvorschrift Fw 190 (Luftwaffe operating instructions); WW2 Aircraft archives
notes: Wartime Luftwaffe placard limit: 750 km/h IAS for Fw 190 A-3 through A-8 (later K and TA-152 allowed 850 km/h with reinforcement). 750 km/h = 405 KIAS = 466 mph. Stored 467 corresponds to mph from US/Allied intelligence summaries (US wartime tech reports converted 750 km/h to ~466 mph). Treat stored as mph: 467 ÷ 1.15078 = 406 KIAS. Original unit: km/h. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: GameBird GB1
source: Game Composites GB1 GameBird Airplane Flight Manual
notes: GB1 GameBird AFM Vne = 234 KIAS. Stored 223 may be from Vno (200) or another value (Vlo/Vle). POH uses knots. Stored value is in knots. No conversion needed; recommend update to canonical 234 KIAS.

name: Grumman F6F-5 Hellcat
source: F6F-5 Pilot's Flight Operating Instructions (US Navy AN 01-85FD-1, 1944-era)
notes: WWII USN piston-aircraft pilot manuals were published in MPH (Navy switched to knots c. 1948 for jets; F6F manuals used mph throughout). Maximum allowable IAS for F6F-5 = 460 mph (some sources cite 415-430 in knots from secondary/simulator data). 460 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 400 KIAS. Original unit: mph.

name: Grumman F8F-2 Bearcat
notes: F8F-2 was a late-1940s USN fighter. The 1945-era US Navy manuals used MPH (knots only became standard mid-1950s). Stored 455 likely mph (max IAS from manual). 455 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 395 KIAS. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM — primary source manual access limited.

name: Kawanishi N1K2-J Shiden-Kai
source: Japanese Naval Air Service technical documents; NASM N1K2-J reference
notes: Japanese IJN aircraft, original operating limits were in km/h. Approximate dive limit ~740 km/h IAS = 400 KIAS = 460 mph. Stored 440 could be mph from US intelligence wartime estimates (rounded). 440 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 382 KIAS. CONFIDENCE LOW — primary IJN documentation hard to access; defer to US wartime estimate as mph.

name: Luscombe 8A
notes: CAR-3 era 1946 aircraft; TCDS A-694 published in MPH. Vne approximately 144 mph (placard speed). Stored matches mph. Needs conversion: 144 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 125 KIAS.

name: MX Aircraft MXS
notes: Modern carbon-fiber unlimited aerobatic aircraft built in Australia. Vne ~275 KIAS per builder specs (experimental category, owner-furnished limits). Modern aerobatic aircraft use knots. No conversion needed.

name: Maule M-7
notes: Modern Maule POH uses KIAS. Vne = 158 KIAS (= 182 mph). Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Messerschmitt Bf 109G-6
notes: Luftwaffe placard Vne: 750 km/h IAS for G-series (early G/G-6); some late-G/K variants increased to 800 km/h. 750 km/h = 405 KIAS = 466 mph. Stored value not yet populated; recommend 405 KIAS. Original unit: km/h.

name: Mitsubishi A6M5 Zero
source: Japanese Imperial Navy Type 0 Carrier Fighter Model 52 manual; wartime intelligence reports
notes: A6M5 Type 0 Model 52 had dive limits around 660 km/h IAS = 356 KIAS = 410 mph. Stored 410 corresponds to mph (US intelligence reports commonly used mph for Japanese aircraft). 410 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 356 KIAS. Original unit km/h; US reports show as mph; canonical KIAS = 356.

name: North American P-51D Mustang
source: P-51D Pilot's Flight Operating Instructions (AAF AN 01-60JE-1)
notes: P-51D Handbook: max IAS at 5000 ft = 505 mph (placard redline). 505 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 439 KIAS. Decreases with altitude (e.g., 400 mph at 20K ft, 260 mph at 40K ft). Stored value not yet populated; recommend 439 KIAS (sea-level placard). Original unit: mph.

name: PT-17 Stearman
source: FAA TCDS A-743 (post-war civil); Stearman PT-17 Pilot's Flight Operating Instructions
notes: Stearman PT-17 TCDS A-743 placard redline = 186 mph (163 kt). Stored 157 does not match — possibly a maximum maneuvering speed or different operational placard. If treating stored as mph: 157 ÷ 1.15078 = 136 KIAS. Recommend canonical 162 KIAS (186 mph) per primary source. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM — value source uncertain.

name: Piper Aztec F
source: Piper PA-23-250 Aztec F Pilot's Operating Handbook
notes: Aztec F (1976-1981) POH lists Vne ~216 KIAS for normally-aspirated; turbo can reach 217 KIAS at 24K ft. Stored 197 does not match either KIAS or MPH directly. Could be older Piper convention (Aztec D used MPH dual). Earlier Aztec D Vne was approximately 227 mph (197 kt). Interpretation: 197 may be knots from a different sub-variant. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM — recommend canonical 216 KIAS for F model.

name: Piper PA-30 Twin Comanche
notes: 1963-1972 PA-30 POH used MPH/CAS markings. Vne redline approximately 230 mph (200 kt). Stored 224 closely matches MPH (within rounding from earlier B-model variants). If treating as mph: 224 ÷ 1.15078 = 195 KIAS. Canonical: 200 KIAS (230 mph). CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Piper PA-44 Seminole
notes: PA-44 Seminole POH Vne = 202 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Pipistrel Alpha Trainer
notes: Modern LSA, POH uses knots. Vne = 135 KIAS (250 km/h). Stored 138 close but slightly off — possibly from Virus/different variant. Original unit: knots/km-h. No conversion needed.

name: Pipistrel Virus
notes: Modern LSA/CS-LSA, POH uses knots. Vne = 163 KIAS at sea level (302 km/h), reduces with altitude. Stored 160 within rounding. No conversion needed.

name: Pitts S-1C
source: Pitts S-1C POH / FAA TCDS A18SO
notes: Pitts S-1C (flat-bottom wing, 1960s-70s era) is older homebuilt; original placard typically in MPH (~174-185 mph Vne). Stored 174 likely mph. 174 ÷ 1.15078 = 151 KIAS. Newer S-1S/T uses 203 mph or knots. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Pitts S-1C-TB
source: Pitts S-1C variant (TB = taper-bottom wing modification)
notes: S-1C-TB shares S-1C airframe limits, original placard in MPH. Stored 174 = mph. Conversion: 151 KIAS. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Pitts S-2C
notes: Modern S-2C (2000s Aviat production), POH uses KIAS. Vne = 204 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Remos GX
notes: Modern LSA, POH uses knots. Vne approximately 145 KIAS (some sources cite 134 kt, others 146 kt depending on revision). Stored 146 within reasonable range. No conversion needed.

name: Robin DR400
notes: DR400 modern POH lists Vne ~270 km/h = 146 KIAS (varies by sub-variant from 270-318 km/h). Stored 162 does not match. Could be from DR400/180R variant (318 km/h = 172 kt) or older transcription. Original unit km/h. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM — recommend verify against specific sub-variant.

name: Robin R3000
notes: R3000 variants Vne ranges from 149 KIAS (R3000 base) to 171 KIAS (R3000/160). Stored 161 falls between. Original unit km/h. POH uses knots. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Socata TB10
source: Socata TB10 Tobago Pilot's Information Manual
notes: Modern French aircraft, POH uses KIAS. Vne = 187 KIAS. Stored 185 close within rounding. No conversion needed.

name: Socata TB20
source: Socata TB20 Trinidad AOPA factsheet; Socata POH
notes: TB20 Trinidad POH lists Vne = 187 KIAS (some specs cite 189 kt). Stored 194 does not match. Could be from TB21 turbo variant. Original unit knots. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Socata TB9
source: Socata TB9 Tampico POH; specs database
notes: TB9 Tampico Vne = 306 km/h = 165 KCAS = 190 mph. Stored 173 does not match either; possibly from Tampico Club variant. Original unit km/h. POH uses knots. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Stinson 108
notes: CAR-3 era 1946-1950 aircraft, TCDS A-767 published in MPH. Vne approximately 162 mph (placard limit). Stored matches mph verbatim. 162 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 141 KIAS.

name: Sukhoi Su-26
source: Sukhoi Su-26M technical specifications; NASM reference
notes: Russian unlimited aerobatic aircraft. Original Soviet placard likely in km/h (~450 km/h = ~243 kt). Modern Western-converted POHs often list 250 KIAS. CONFIDENCE LOW — primary Russian docs hard to access. Assume stored is knots.

name: Supermarine Spitfire Mk IX
source: Spitfire IX/XI/XVI Pilot's Notes (RAF Air Publication 1565J,L,P)
notes: RAF Pilot's Notes for Spitfire Mk IX list maximum dive speed = 450 mph IAS (sea level to 20,000 ft), decreasing to 380 mph at 30K ft. 450 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 391 KIAS. Stored value not yet populated; recommend 391 KIAS. Original unit: mph (RAF used mph throughout WWII).

name: T-6A Texan II
source: T-6A Texan II Flight Manual (USAF AFMAN 11-248); TCDS T00012WI
notes: Modern military trainer, AFM uses knots. Vne (Vmo) = 316 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: T-6B Texan II
source: T-6B Texan II Flight Manual (Naval Air Training)
notes: Identical airframe to T-6A; Vne = 316 KIAS. Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Taylorcraft BC-12D
source: FAA TCDS A-696 Taylorcraft BC-12D; AOPA factsheet
notes: CAR-3 1946 era aircraft, TCDS A-696 published in MPH. Common spec: Vne = 140 mph. Stored 136 close but not exact — possibly a more conservative placard or different sub-variant year. Treat as mph: 136 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 118 KIAS. Canonical via AOPA: 140 mph = 122 KIAS. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Tecnam P2002
notes: P2002 JF/Sierra POH Vne = 138 KIAS (141 KCAS). Some MOD versions list 141-142 KIAS. Stored 155 does not match. POH uses knots. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM — recommend verify against specific variant; may need correction.

name: Tecnam P2006T
notes: Modern Italian twin, POH uses KIAS. Vne = 167 KIAS (168 KCAS). Stored 160 does not match. POH uses knots. Recommend canonical 167 KIAS.

name: Van's RV-10
notes: Van's RV-10 Vne = 200 KTAS (note: Van's specifies as TAS, not IAS). Stored 200 matches knots. No conversion needed but flutter limit is TAS-referenced.

name: Van's RV-12
notes: RV-12 POH Vne = 136 KIAS (below 16,000 ft). Stored matches. No conversion needed.

name: Van's RV-14A
notes: Van's RV-14 official Vne = 200 KTAS (230 mph TAS) at sea level. Stored 210 does not match. Could be older or alternative placard. Recommend canonical 200 KIAS/KTAS.

name: Van's RV-6
source: Van's Aircraft RV-6/6A airspeed indicator markings; Van's documentation
notes: Older Van's RV models (RV-4, RV-6, RV-8) per Van's chart list Vne in statute MPH (~210 mph for RV-6/6A). Stored 200 close but not exact. Treating as mph: 200 ÷ 1.15078 = 174 KIAS. Canonical per Van's chart: 210 mph = 182 KIAS. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Van's RV-8
source: Van's Aircraft RV-8 specifications; airspeed indicator markings chart
notes: Van's RV-8 Vne = 200 KTAS (230 mph TAS). Stored 220 could be mph (matches Van's older mph-only ASI). Treating as mph: 220 ÷ 1.15078 = 191 KIAS. Canonical: 200 KIAS. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Van's RV-9A
notes: Van's RV-9/9A Vne = 180 KTAS (= 210 mph TAS). Some owners report 182 KIAS on ASI. Stored 189 close to 182 KIAS but not exact. Could be from a specific airspeed indicator marked in mph (=189 mph = 164 KIAS). Likely stored is mph: 189 ÷ 1.15078 = 164 KIAS. Canonical: 182 KIAS. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Vought F4U-4 Corsair
source: F4U-4 Corsair Pilot's Flight Operating Instructions (US Navy)
notes: 1944-era USN pilot manual used MPH (Navy switched to knots in early 1950s for jet aircraft; piston manuals still in mph). Maximum allowable IAS for F4U-4 ~446 mph at low altitude. Stored matches. 446 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 388 KIAS. Original unit: mph.

name: Yakovlev Yak-3
source: Yak-3 specifications; Soviet VVS documentation summaries
notes: Soviet WWII fighter, original limits in km/h. Approximate dive limit ~700 km/h = 378 KIAS = 435 mph. Stored 435 matches mph (US/Western secondary sources). 435 mph ÷ 1.15078 = 378 KIAS. CONFIDENCE LOW — primary Soviet docs hard to access; recommend canonical 378 KIAS.

name: Zivko Edge 540
notes: Modern US-built unlimited aerobatic aircraft. Builder specs cite ~426 km/h (230 kt) max speed but some sources cite higher Vne limits up to 270 kt. Stored 270 likely in knots (modern aerobatic convention). CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Zlin Savage
source: Zlin Aviation Savage Cub 912 Flight Manual
notes: Modern Italian-built LSA (despite the Zlin name). Real-world ASI displays in km/h, but POH lists limits in both knots and km/h. Vne approximately 130 KIAS (~240 km/h). Stored matches knots. CONFIDENCE MEDIUM.

name: Zlin Z-242L
notes: Czech-built aerobatic trainer. Original Z-242L flight manual lists speeds in km/h. Max speed 236 km/h (127 kt). Stored 216 may be max cruise speed (Vno) in km/h = 117 KIAS, or Vne placard value of ~216 km/h. Actual placard Vne should be ~280 km/h (151 KIAS). CONFIDENCE MEDIUM — stored value seems inconsistent; if treating stored as km/h: 216 ÷ 1.852 = 117 KIAS, but recommend verify against actual POH for correct Vne. If stored is intended as kt, then 216 KIAS is plausible only if mismatched units.
